issues: Choice c's arbitrary division by two is not a pedagogically plausible misconception; it functions as contrived filler on a four-choice card.
learnerValue: Directly tests the numerical relationship between relative formula mass and the mass of one mole in grams.
Reinforces that stoichiometric mass calculations require a balanced symbol equation.
Tests the complete mass-to-moles-to-whole-number-ratio balancing process.
Distinguishes the limiting reactant from an excess reactant or simply the reactant with less mass.
Securely tests oxidation and reduction through electron loss and gain.
Targets the defining distinction between complete and partial acid ionisation.
Integrates ion charge, electrode, electron transfer and redox terminology in one retrieval task.
Tests the correct order and operation in the bond-energy calculation.
Retrieves the central counteracting response described by Le Chatelier’s Principle.
Tests the direction of the equilibrium response to increased reactant concentration.
Contrasts the opposing effects of increased temperature on endothermic and exothermic equilibria.
Tests both pressure-change directions and their relationship to gaseous molecule counts.
Clearly distinguishes the organisms and metal-compound-containing products involved in phytomining and bioleaching.
